question:
Will Alibaba’s Qwen 3 Max win the NOF1.ai competition?

Description: This market will resolve according to the AI model listed below that shows the highest account value on the NOF1.ai leaderboard (https://nof1.ai/) when the competition concludes, currently scheduled for November 3, 2025, 5:00 PM ET.

Results from the leaderboard displayed on https://nof1.ai/, showing the total account value for each model, will be used to resolve this market.

Any AI model whose total account value equals zero before the check time will be considered eliminated and may resolve “No” immediately.

If two or more models are tied for the highest account value at the time of resolution, the tiebreaker will be determined first by the higher Win Rate. If a tie still remains, resolution will be based on the alphabetical order of the models’ company names as listed.

If the leaderboard is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard becomes available and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If the leaderboard becomes permanently unavailable, or if the competition does not finish, this market will resolve based on the latest reliable reports available.

evidences.title: Qwen 3 Max in Nof1's Alpha Arena live AI crypto trading experiment
evidences.content: The Alpha Arena project organized by nof1 tests how advanced AI models perform in live crypto trading. Each system starts with $10,000 in real capital to trade perpetual contracts on Hyperliquid under identical conditions, with participants including DeepSeek Chat V3.1, Qwen 3 Max, Claude Sonnet 4.5, Grok 4, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and GPT-5. The goal is to maximize risk-adjusted returns.
evidences.query: Alibaba Qwen 3 Max NOF1.ai leaderboard competitors GPT Claude Gemini Grok DeepSeek model roster

evidences.title: Nof1 discusses goals for Alpha Arena and future iterations
evidences.content: Nof1 says Alpha Arena is intended as a transparent step toward understanding how AI behaves in real financial markets, including what conditions help autonomous systems learn, compete fairly, and add value. The company also says it is planning a future season with more features, an improved prompt and harness, and greater statistical rigor.

evidences.title: Qwen 3 Max in Nof1's Alpha Arena crypto trading competition
evidences.content: Alpha Arena, organized by US research firm nof1, is a benchmark testing advanced AI models in live crypto trading. Each participant receives $10,000 in capital to trade perpetual contracts on Hyperliquid under identical conditions, with the goal of maximizing risk-adjusted returns. Participants include DeepSeek Chat V3.1, Qwen 3 Max, Claude Sonnet 4.5, Grok 4, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and GPT-5.

evidences.title: Qwen3 Max Thinking - Intelligence, Performance & Price Analysis
evidences.content: No, Qwen3 Max Thinking is proprietary. The model weights are not publicly available. Qwen3 Max Thinking is a proprietary model and Alibaba has not disclosed the model size or parameter count. Qwen3 Max Thinking achieves a score of 32 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. This composite benchmark evaluates models across reasoning, knowledge, mathematics, and coding. Check the providers page for current API availability.View providers Visit the providers page to see availability.Compare providers [...] Qwen3 Max Thinking was created by Alibaba. Qwen3 Max Thinking scores 32 (estimated) on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, placing it well above average among other reasoning models in a similar price tier (median: 14). Yes, Qwen3 Max Thinking is a reasoning model. It uses extended thinking or chain-of-thought reasoning to work through complex problems before providing an answer. Qwen3 Max Thinking supports text input. Qwen3 Max Thinking supports text output. No, Qwen3 Max Thinking does not support image input. It can only process text. No, Qwen3 Max Thinking is not multimodal. It only supports text input. Qwen3 Max Thinking has a context window of 260k tokens. This...

[...] ## Conclusion Qwen3-Max-Thinking represents a significant moment in the AI landscape: a Chinese model definitively outperforming Western frontier models on the hardest reasoning benchmarks — at a fraction of the cost. evidences.title: AI trading in real markets
evidences.content: Nof1 is exploring how to make markets understandable for autonomous agents: what conditions and interfaces help them learn, compete fairly, and add value without privileged access or manipulation. The project studies what capabilities and safeguards are needed for AI trading systems, with Alpha Arena presented as an early, transparent step toward a broader vision. The team says it is planning a future season with more features, an improved prompt and harness, and greater statistical rigor.

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation: Introduction
evidences.content: api_key = <your_api_key> secret_key = <your_secret_key> recvWindow = <recvWindow> uri_path = "/api/v3/rateLimit/order" data = { "recvWindow": recvWindow, "timestamp": int(round(time.time() 1000)) } result = binanceus_request(uri_path, data, api_key, secret_key) print("GET {}: {}".format(uri_path, result)) ``` > Response ``` [ { "rateLimitType": "ORDERS", "interval": "SECOND", "intervalNum": 10, "limit": 100, "count": 0 }, { "rateLimitType": "ORDERS", "interval": "DAY", "intervalNum": 1, "limit": 200000, "count": 0 } ] ``` `GET /api/v3/rateLimit/order (HMAC SHA256)` Get the current trade order count rate limits for all time intervals. Weight: 20 Parameters: [...] | Status | Description | --- | | `NEW` | The order has been accepted by the engine. | | `PARTIALLY_FILLED` | A part of the order has been filled. | | `FILLED` | The order has been completed. | | `CANCELED` | The order has been canceled by the user. | | `PENDING_CANCEL` | Currently unused | | `REJECTED` | The order was not accepted by the engine and not processed. | | `EXPIRED` | The order was canceled according to the order type's rules (e.g. LIMIT FOK orders with no fill, LIMIT IOC or MARKET orders that partially fill)...

evidences.title: AI trading in real markets
evidences.content: Alpha Arena Nof1 A decade ago, DeepMind revolutionized AI research. Their key insight was that choosing the right environment – games – would lead to rapid progress in frontier AI. At Nof1, we believe financial markets are the best training environment for the next era of AI. They are the ultimate world-modeling engine and the only benchmark that gets harder as AI gets smarter. Instead of games, we're using markets to train new base models that create their own training data indefinitely. We're using techniques like open-ended learning and large-scale RL to handle the complexity of markets, the final boss. If this resonates, we're hiring: engineers, researchers, founders, original thinkers. If you're excited to build AlphaZero for the real world, get in touch. [...] “Capital allocation is the discipline through which intelligence converges with truth.”

evidences.title: Summary of Failed Orders in Binance Futures | Binance Futures,How to use Binance Futures,Binance Futures order failure
evidences.content: Please note that position limits vary by symbol and the chosen leverage. You should stay within this limit to ensure your orders can be successfully executed. When closing the position, it might also fail for exceeding the position size limit due to having open orders. Please cancel open orders and close the position again if encountering the error: “Order rejected due to exceeding the maximum allowable quantity at current leverage. To close position, please cancel the open orders and try again later.” #### 3. Your order did not meet the minimum notional value Please refer to USDⓈ-Margined Futures Contract Specifications for the minimum notional value. #### 4. You placed a [Reduce-only] order [...] Here are some possible reasons for order failure when trading on Binance Futures. #### 1. Your margin balance is insufficient You have other open orders that share the same margin, or the order amount exceeds the position amount. This means you need to add more margin to open the position. The lower the leverage, the higher the required margin balance. You can reduce the leverage before placing the order again. #### 2. Your order exceeds the position size limit When placing an order,...

evidences.title: Alpha Arena: The Most Degen AI Benchmark Yet
evidences.content: Sitemap Open in app Sign up Sign in enough to ask: “What if we benchmarked AI models by letting them gamble?” This isn’t a hypothetical thought experiment or another sanitized academic benchmark where models compete on static datasets. This is live ammunition in financial form, deployed by nof1.ai — a company so new its LinkedIn profile still has that fresh startup smell — testing whether Grok 4, GPT-5, and Claude Sonnet 4.5 have what it takes to make you rich. Or, more likely, to demonstrate that artificial intelligence plus autonomous trading equals artificial losses, just much faster than human traders can achieve. ## The audacity of benchmarking by bankruptcy
